Simon’s Public NAO Notebook

Analyzing aggregate qPCR data from Fan et al. 2023

Author

Simon Grimm

Published

March 25, 2024

Code 
# Importing packages
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from collections import defaultdict
import random
import math

qPCR kits

Code 
with open("data/fan_2023_supplement_Table_S1.tsv") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

    for line in lines:
        bits = line.split("\t")
        target_sub_table = True
        # if bits[0].count("different") == 1:
        #    target_sub_table = True
        #    print("worked")
        #    continue
        if target_sub_table == True:
            print("hello")
            (
                qpcr_kit,
                mean_2e3,
                sd_2e3,
                n_labs_2e3,
                _,
                mean_1e3,
                sd_1e3,
                n_labs_1e3,
                _,
                mean_5e2,
                sd_5e2,
                n_labs_5e2,
                _,
                mean_2e2,
                sd_2e2,
                n_labs_2e2,
            ) = bits
            print(
                qpcr_kit,
                mean_2e3,
                sd_2e3,
                n_labs_2e3,
                mean_1e3,
                sd_1e3,
                n_labs_1e3,
                mean_5e2,
                sd_5e2,
                n_labs_5e2,
                mean_2e2,
                sd_2e2,
                n_labs_2e2,
            )
            continue
hello
RT-PCR kits 2.0×103 copies/mL   1.0×103 copies/mL   5.0×102 copies/mL   2.0×102 copies/mL  

hello
 mean SD Lab No. mean SD Lab No. mean SD Lab No. mean SD Lab No.
